THE COURT VACATES THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT FINDS HERTZ FAILED TO CARRY ITS BURDEN UNDER CCP 2025.450(B)(2). THE COURT FINDS HERTZ'S POSITION IN THIS MOTION IS NOT SUBSTANTIALLY JUSTIFIED. THE COURT SANCTIONS HERTZ IN THE AMOUNT OF $1000.00 PAYABLE TO THE COURT CLERK TO BE PAID NO LATER THAN 3/24/25 AND A RECEIPT POSTED IN THE REGISTER OF ACTIONS NO LATER THAN 4/2/25. MR. DAVIS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS COMPLAINANT THE HERTZ CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND CROSS COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT THE HERTZ CORPORATION'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED, EXCEPT THAT THE COURT WILL NOT "DEEM" THE AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED. INSTEAD, MOVING PARTY MUST FILE ITS FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT, WHICH MUST MATCH THE PROPOSED AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT, WITHIN 5 COURT DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND DROP A HARD PAPER COPY IN DEPT. 302'S INBOX FOR THE COURT'S REVIEW AND SIGNATURE. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Attendance At Depositions Of The Defendant And Cross-Defendant Sunrise'S Person(S) Most Qualified, Set Two And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,250.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Supporting Delcaration Of John W. Ranucci
Off Calendar Feb-26-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Robert Davis, Esq. 925-953-9914 MG+M The Law Firm for Defendant Sunrise Automotive
Olivia Palmieri, Esq. (562) 983-2500 FORD, WALKER, HAGGERTY & BEHAR for Defendant/Moving party Hertz Corporation.

9:38am At the first calling of the case, the Court passes the matter due to connectivity and audio issues of Mr. Davis.

9:46am
ARGUED. Mr. Robert Davis contests the tentative ruling and argues. Ms. Palmieri responds to the court's inquiry re CCP 2025.450(b)(2). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT THE HERTZ CORPORATION's Motion For Order Compelling Attendance At Depositions Of The Defendant And Cross-Defendant Sunrise'S Person(S) Most Qualified, Set Two And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,250.00.

The Court vacates the tentative ruling. The Court finds Hertz failed to carry its burden under CCP 2025.450(b)(2). The Court finds Hertz's position in this motion is not substantially justified. The Court sanctions Hertz in the amount of $1000.00 payable to the court clerk to be paid no later than 3/24/25 and a receipt posted in the register of actions no later than 4/2/25. Mr. Davis to prepare a proposed order approved as to form.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS COMPLAINANT THE HERTZ CORPORATION's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND CROSS COMPLAINT.

Defendant and Cross-Complainant The Hertz Corporation's unopposed Motion for Leave to Amend Cross-Complaint is GRANTED, except that the court will not "deem" the amended cross-complaint filed. Instead, Moving Party must file its first amended cross-complaint, which must match the proposed amended cross-complaint, within 5 court days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and drop a hard paper copy in Dept. 302's inbox for the court's review and signature.
